sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 15.97 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.15)

statement.latex: Let$p$be a prime. A group$G$satisfies the$p$-mini\-mal condition\/ if there are no infinite descending chains$G_1>G_2>$of subgroups of$G$such that each difference$G_i G_{i+1}$contains a$p$-ele\-ment (S.\,N.\,Chernikov). Suppose that a locally finite group$G$satisfies the$p$-mini\-mal condition and has a subnormal series each of whose factors is finite or a$p'$-group. Is it true that all$p$-ele\-me...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
